text: Sedentary men experience a VO2max decline of approximately 12% per decade with aging.
Master endurance athletes who maintain regular vigorous training experience a VO2max decline of approximately 5.5% per decade, roughly half the rate of sedentary individuals.
Master athletes who continue vigorous endurance training maintain higher absolute VO2max values (~52 ml/kg/min) compared to sedentary age-matched peers (~31 ml/kg/min) into their late 60s.
population: master endurance athletes vs sedentary men aged ~60-70

text: Continued endurance exercise training may attenuate the age-related decline in maximal heart rate, with master athletes showing no significant decline over 8 years while sedentary peers lost ~8 beats/min.
population: master endurance athletes vs sedentary men aged ~60

text: Maximal oxygen pulse (VO2/heart beat) declines with aging in both trained and sedentary individuals, indicating reduced stroke volume or arteriovenous O2 difference regardless of training status.
population: master endurance athletes and sedentary men aged ~60-70
